Off Calendar Mar-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, LP, SOUTH SAN FRANCISCO I, LLC AND GEORGE V. CRESSON III'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN PCS' CROSS-COMPLAINT AND ON THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF'S FIFTH AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
James Madow (no card provided) plaintiff appearing in propria persona; Devin C. Contreau of Ropers Majeski Kohn & Bentley (650.364.8200) appearing for Post Construction Services, LP, South San Francisco I LLC, George V. Cresson III; Kevin Brodehl of Wendel Rosen Black & Dean LLP (510.834.6600) for South Bay Real Estate Commerce Group, LLC

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for summary adjudication as to rescission is denied. It is an improper motion to reconsider in that it requests exactly the same relief on the basis of exactly the same document as the prior motion. Although premised on a different signature line in that document, the argument was available to and could and should have been raised by defendants in their prior motion. In any event, there remains a triable issue of fact as to the effectiveness of the attorney's signature in light of the arguable ambiguity as to the party on whose behalf the letter was sent given the party signature. Order signed. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not reported

Defendants George Cresson & South Bay Real Estate Commerce Group, Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SOUTH BAY REAL ESTATE COMMERCE GROUP, LLC AND GEORGE V. CRESSON III'S JOINT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances: James Madow (no card provided) plaintiff appearing in propria persona; Devin C. Contreau of Ropers Majeski Kohn & Bentley (650.364.8200) appearing for Post Construction Services, LP, South San Francisco I LLC, George V. Cresson III; Kevin Brodehl of Wendel Rosen Black & Dean LLP (510.834.6600) for South Bay Real Estate Commerce Group, LLC

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for summary adjudication is granted with respect to the claim for breach of fiduciary duty because the gravamen of the claim is injury to the entity, and plaintiff lacks standing to sue on the entity's behalf for injuries to the entity. Injuries, if any, to the plaintiff are indirect. The motion is denied as to the request for declaratory relief. There remain issues for trial at least because the trial court, considering all the circumstances, could agree to allow amendment to address the effect of the more recent actions, and there remain issues as to prior actions notwithstanding plaintiff's prior statements. Defendants to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CCP 437 c(g). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, LP, SOUTH SAN FRANCISCO I, LLC and GEORGE CRESSON's AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff acknowledges that the first, second, third and fourth causes of action are substantially re-pled from the Third Amended Complaint, and thus, notwithstanding the filing of the fourth amended complaint, in the interests of judicial economy and substantial justice, the court addresses the motion on the merits. Plaintiff has presented admissible evidence showing that there are triable issues whether he received the June 21, 2012 letter prior to his purported revocation of the offer of rescission and whether his dismissal of the tenth cause of action was effectively a revocation. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
